prompt: Pattern.compile throws PatternSyntaxException. What is the bug?
choices: Pattern.compile cannot be called with escaped sequences like \\d
The regex "\\d+(" has an unclosed capture group '('; every '(' must have a matching ')', causing PatternSyntaxException at compile time
Pattern.compile must be used with Matcher.find(), not Matcher.matches()
The + quantifier requires a preceding character class in the pattern
explanation: An opening '(' in a regex must be matched by a closing ')'. "\d+(" has an unclosed group, which is a syntax error. Pattern.compile throws PatternSyntaxException. Fix: Pattern.compile("\\d+") or Pattern.compile("(\\d+)") with the closing parenthesis.
